title: asperula aristata var. glabrescens
intro: Asperula aristata var. glabrescens is a variety of the Asperula aristata plant, also known as mountain wood sorrel. This perennial herb is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, and is characterized by its yellow flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its astringent and anti-inflammatory effects, which are said to help soothe digestive issues and reduce swelling. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the Asperula aristata var. glabrescens can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to leverage its health benefits, such as reducing fever and promoting wound healing.
